summary: World Cup debutants (FIFA 50th) who finished second to Iran in qualifying. Timur Kapadze led them there but has since been replaced by Ballon d'Or winner Fabio Cannavaro, under whom they've leaned defensive — just five goals conceded in his first seven matches, anchored by Manchester City's Abdukodir Khusanov.
style: Not expansive — often three central defenders dropping into a 5-4-1 without the ball, with the opposition retreating into their defensive third as the trigger to push more players (including defenders) forward. In possession, encouraged to play quick passes through the middle or over the top to the forwards, even when shorter options exist (the build-up to Shomurodov's goal vs Kuwait).

counterReliance.note: Defensive 5-4-1 block; quick vertical passing to the forwards

strengths: Strong, organised defence (5 conceded in Cannavaro's first 7) led by Khusanov
Direct, vertical supply through the middle or over the top to a proven goalscorer
Set-piece threat (two of three goals vs Gabon from dead balls)
weaknesses: Limited expansiveness — relies on its block and moments rather than control
Debutant step-up; a new manager bedding in close to the tournament

keyPlayers.name: Abdukodir Khusanov
keyPlayers.note: The defensive lynchpin of the debutants — pace and quality at the back that the whole system leans on

keyPlayers.name: Eldor Shomurodov
keyPlayers.note: All-time top scorer (44 in 91); coming off 23 goals in 2025-26 for İstanbul Başakşehir — the outlet for the vertical balls

tactics: Sit in a 5-4-1, stay compact, and break vertically to Shomurodov with quick passes through or over the top; threaten from set pieces. Cannavaro's defensive structure is the foundation for a debutant hoping to frustrate stronger sides.
notes: Coach change since qualifying — Kapadze out, Fabio Cannavaro in.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group K (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
